description_blurb: The FBI's 62-HQ-83894 case file includes investigative records, eyewitness testimonies, and public reports concerning Unidentified Flying Objects and flying discs documented between June 1947 and July 1968. The records include high-profile incident accounts, photographic evidence from sites like Oak Ridge, TN, and technical proposals regarding potential propulsion systems. Additional topics include convention programs, researcher accounts, and extensive media coverage from the period.  This file is partially posted on FBI vault with more redactions and some pages missing.  Included here is the complete case file with several newly declassified pages and only minor redactions.
title: 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_9
summary: This section of FBI case file 62-HQ-83894 contains internal memoranda from late 1957 and early 1958. It details reports of flying discs in Cuba, described as silent and hat-shaped, and summarizes other US sightings from October-November 1957, including objects resembling the moon with blue flames, fiery objects, and objects causing car engines to stop. The Air Force resolved these US sightings as non-authentic. The document also covers the FBI's review of the "Inter Continental Aerial Research Foundation" (ICARF), a civilian organization seeking public assistance and photographic evidence for UFOs, believing the Air Force is concealing information. The FBI decided against further investigation into ICARF.
reported_object_description: Flying disc, shape of a man's hat, larger than ordinary airplanes, completely silent, looked like the moon, trailing a blue flame that pulsed like a heart, fiery object, huge object.
reported_behavior: Hovering, disappeared at a very high speed, landed, sped off into space, flashing across the southern skies, flying over, caused engines in automobiles to stop.
official_resolution: Air Force investigated Vernal, Utah reports with negative results. Air Force resolved other serious US sightings as "nonauthentic or there was no basis for the sightings." FBI took no further action on specific reports or the Inter Continental Aerial Research Foundation.
bust_reasoning: The document contains several sensational reports, including silent, fast-moving discs and alleged landings, but explicitly states that the Air Force 'resolved as being nonauthentic or there was no basis for the sightings' for the US cases. The Cuban reports, while describing unusual objects, are uninvestigated newspaper clippings. There is no multi-sensor corroboration or official investigation within the document that validates anomalous behavior, and the Air Force's internal debunking of the US cases is a strong factor for a higher bust score.

# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_9 - Page 4

STANDARD FORM NO. 64
Office Memorandum • UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT

TO : Director, FBI [Handwritten: Jm] [Handwritten: eog]
FROM : Legal Attache, Havana, Cuba [Handwritten: O]
DATE: November 20, 1957
SUBJECT: REPORTS OF FLYING DISCS
FOREIGN MISCELLANEOUS

The following information is submitted as of interest
to the Bureau in the event reports of flying discs are still desired.

[Handwritten: Informant Informant Cuba]
The Havana daily newspaper "Diario de la Marina" November 12,
1957, carried a dispatch datelined November 11, 1957, at Pinar del Rio,
Cuba, reporting that JOSE MARIA NIETO and CARMELO GUZMAN, who had
just arrived in that town from the Matahambre Mines in Pinar del Rio
Province, claimed that they had seen a flying disc in the shape of a
man's hat hovering over the general area of Matahambre for several
minutes. They claimed that this disc was larger than ordinary airplanes
normally seen in that area and that it was completely silent in its
flight. They said that it disappeared at a very high speed towards
the sea. There was no further descriptive information furnished in
this newspaper article. It is noted that Matahambre is located
approximately 90 miles to the west of Havana. No other reports
corroborating the above were received and no inquiry has been made
by this office.

JJW:ptm
(3)

[Stamp: INDEXED-46]
[Handwritten: 34]
[Handwritten: ek]

[Stamp: 60 NOV 27 1957]

# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_9 - Page 6

STANDARD FORM NO. 64
Office Memorandum - UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT

TO : MR. A. H. BELMONT [Handwritten: 11-13-57]
FROM : MR. R. R. ROACH
SUBJECT: UNIDENTIFIED FLYING OBJECTS (FLYING SAUCERS)

[Right-hand column routing list]
Tolson
Nichols
Boardman
Belmont
Mohr
Parsons
Rosen
Tamm
Trotter
Nease
Tele. Room
Holloman
Gandy

[Handwritten notes on right side]
[Bowen 78]
[Rop 15]
[Informed Whiten]

Ever since the Russians released "Sputnik" there has been a great increase in the number of "flying saucers" and other unidentified flying objects (UFO) reportedly seen by people all over the U. S. On October 10, 1957, the Air Force was flooded with calls and the newspapers were full of reports that an unknown object described as "looked like the moon" and trailing a "blue flame that pulsed like a heart" had landed in the vicinity of Vernal, Utah. The Air Force immediately investigated these reports with negative results, inasmuch as no object was located nor was there any evidence of an object having fallen in Utah.

Within the past two weeks reports in these cases have increased tremendously and some of the more serious have been described as follows: An object had landed in Nebraska with six people aboard, the persons had talked to a Nebraska farmer and then sped off into space; a firey object was seen flashing across the southern skies from Albany, Georgia, to Miami, Florida; a Coast Guard cutter had sighted a huge object flying over the Gulf of Mexico, and persons in the Southwestern states while driving their cars have allegedly seen UFO's that caused the engines in their automobiles to stop. In view of the number of these reports, the Bureau's Liaison Representative discussed these various sightings with officials of Air Force Intel ligence and was advised that all the cases mentioned above have for all practical purposes been resolved as being nonauthentic or there was no basis for the sightings. Investigating details that the Air Force had out in the field investigating these matters have not submitted their complete reports but have advised that they are all more or less negative. The Air Force is following these sightings closely and all reports are submitted to the Air Technical and Intelligence Center, at Wright-Patterson Air Force Base in Ohio where they are evaluated and analyzed. In the event any of the future reports appear to be authentic, the Air Force will immediately notify the Bureau, keeping in mind our particular interest in matters concerning espionage and sabotage.

[Handwritten note in red ink on right side: eh]

WTW:jlk (7)
1 - Mr. Belmont
1 - Mr. Branigan
1 - Mr. Baumgardner
1 - Mr. Whitson

On December 20, 1957, Mr. B. B. Watson,

signed by R. T. Stone. This letter bears the letterhead

the upper right corner there is typed "MISSOURI FILTER
CENTER, Robert T. Stone, Director, 10717 E. 27th Terr.,
Independence, Missouri."

The letter furnished by Mr. Watson is as follows:

"Dear Editor:

"The enclosed notice is self-explanatory. We
would like to request this public service in the
name of Truth, which the newspapers of our great
countries so proudly defend and uphold.

# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_9 - Page 10

RECORDED - 83

January 23, 1958

Director, Federal Bureau of Investigation
The Inspector General
Department of the Air Force
Washington 25, D.C.

Dear Sir:

Reference is made to the letter from the Department of the Air Force dated January 16, 1958, concerning the [illegible] of [illegible]



# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_9 - Page 11

Director of Special Investigations

"As a civilian body of investigators, we hope to unravel the snarled mess that shrouds the U.F.O. question, and with the help of the newspapers and the public, where can we fail?"

"We will be grateful if you will publish the enclosed appeal on the front page of your newspaper, in a conspicuous enough manner that no one will fail to notice it. It is the public we must depend upon to get thoroughly organized, and with this co-operation we are certain that we can find the answer to the riddle of the 'Flying Saucers.'"

"If a Filter Center has been established in your state or province, this form letter will carry its address which should replace the headquarters address given on the enclosed 'Public Notice.' If no such filter center address is given, then the original headquarters address should be used."

"We wish to thank you for any assistance you may be able to give us in this respect. Later established filter center addresses will be sent to you for public attention in the event of unusual sightings, that may come to your attention."

"Respectfully,"

/S/ R. T. STONE
"For the Inter Continental Aerial Research Foundation"

Attached to the letter was the notice which it was requested be published. This notice is as follows:

- 2 -



# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_9 - Page 12

Director of Special Investigations





# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_9 - Page 13

Director of Special Investigations

"All persons interested in setting up observer posts who can devote from one to three hours a day on skywatch, and who can be relied upon to have their cameras loaded and ready to photograph these objects should immediately contact us for full information. Adult Skywatch clubs, high school groups, science clubs, industrial groups, and individuals in general are all particularly asked to join us in our search for the answer to the UFOs.

"Contact us at the address above."

"INTER CONTINENTAL AERIAL RESEARCH FOUNDATION"

"FORM OP-2"

Also attached was a three inch by five inch plain card with the handwriting "Please confirm publication or return forms with reason for refusal. Thank you." Signed "Missouri Filter Center; ICARF."

Mr. Watson stated that this is a peculiar request and therefore he was bringing it to the attention of the Federal Bureau of Investigation as he questioned the motives behind this organization's purpose.

No further action in this matter is contemplated by this Bureau. Should additional information be received, however, it will be forwarded promptly to you. The files of this Bureau contain no information identifiable with Robert T. Stone or the Inter Continental Aerial Research Foundation.

- 4 -

Mr. B. B. WATSON, City Editor, Hannibal Courier-Post, Hannibal, Missouri, on 12/20/57 advised the Resident Agent at Hannibal that he was in receipt of an undated letter signed R. T. STONE. This letter bears the letterhead Inter-Continental Aerial Research Foundation and in the upper right corner there is typed the following "MISSOURI FILTER CENTER, Robert T. Stone, Director, 10717 E. 27th Terr., Independence, Missouri". Below this in pencil is a notation made by Mr. WATSON which is "Air Defense Identification Zoning."

The letter is as follows:

"Dear Editor:

"The enclosed notice is self-explanatory. We would like to request this public service in the name of Truth, which the newspapers of our great countries so proudly defend and uphold.

"As a civilian body of investigators, we hope to unravel the snarled mess that shrouds the U.F.O. question, and with the help of the newspapers and the public, where can we fail?

"We will be grateful if you will publish the enclosed appeal on the front page of your newspaper, in a conspicuous enough manner that no one will fail to notice it. It is the public we must depend upon to get thoroughly organized, and with this co-operation we are certain that we can find the answer to the riddle of the 'Flying Saucers.'

"If a filter center has been established in your state or province, this form letter will carry its address which should replace the Headquarters address given on the enclosed 'Public Notice.' If no such"

"We wish to thank you for any assistance you may be able to give us in this respect. Later established filter center addresses will be sent to you for public attention in the event of unusual sightings, that may come to your attention."

Attached to this letter [handwritten: which] is [handwritten: we] ~~the~~ notice. It was requested to be published. This notice is as follows:

"Inter Continental Aerial Research Foundation"

"NOTICE!"
"To All Concerned:"

"If anyone has seen any unidentified flying objects--known also as flying saucers--or knows anyone who has seen them since January 1st, 1957, please contact us immediately, giving full names and addresses of the witnesses to these sightings."

"We are in the process of setting up UFO Observer Posts all over the United States, Canada, Europe, Alaska, Australia, Japan, England, Africa, and South America. And each state, province or region will have its own filter center which will serve as a receiving station for all UFO reports and other associated data, a distribution point for headquarters, and as a general secretary for ICARF."

"Our filter-observer network is expanding by leaps and bounds, but we are still far from our goal."

"The Air Force investigation has either gone underground, or else they have the answer and are hiding the fact from"

- 2 -



# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_9 - Page 17

SL 62-0

the public. The fact remains that millions of people continue to see these UFOs and the public still doesn't know what they are.

"It is our purpose in organizing ICARF to find the answer to the UFO mystery, and give the facts to the public. And in order to do this we will need the assistance of every person concerned. Persons with movie and still cameras are particularly needed, for the emphasis is on photographic data, despite the fact that sightings with binoculars and telescopes, sound detector and ham radio stations are also very important in the process of our research.

"All persons interested in setting up observer posts who can devote from one to three hours a day on skywatch, and who can be relied upon to have their cameras loaded and ready to photograph these objects should immediately contact us for full information. Adult Skywatch clubs, high school groups, science clubs, industrial groups and individuals in general are all particularly asked to join us in our search for the answer to the UFOs.

"Contact us at the address above."

"INTER CONTINENTAL AERIAL
RESEARCH FOUNDATION"

"FORM OP-2"

Also attached [handwritten: was] is a 3 in. by 5 in. plain
card with the handwriting "Please confirm publication
or return forms with reason for refusal. Thank you."
Signed "Missouri Filter Center; ICARF".

Mr. WATSON stated that this is a peculiar
request and therefore he was bringing it to the
attention of the FBI as he questioned the motives
behind this organization's purpose.

The original letters are being submitted
to the Director.

Kansas City Division is requested to
check its indices on ROBERT T. STONE and the Inter
Continental Aerial Research Foundation, and conduct
such inquiry as it deems appropriate.

Inasmuch as there is no further investigation,
in the St. Louis area, Kansas City is being considered
the Office of Origin.

- RUC -

# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_9 - Page 20

INTER CONTINENTAL
AERIAL RESEARCH
FOUNDATION

NOTICE!
TO ALL CONCERNED:

IF ANYONE HAS SEEN ANY UNIDENTIFIED FLYING OBJECTS--KNOWN ALSO AS FLYING SAUCERS--OR KNOWS ANYONE WHO HAS SEEN THEM SINCE JANUARY 1ST, 1957, PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, GIVING FULL NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF THE WITNESSES TO THESE SIGHTINGS.

WE ARE IN THE PROCESS OF SETTING UP UFO OBSERVER POSTS ALL OVER THE UNITED STATES, CANADA, EUROPE, ALASKA, AUSTRALIA, JAPAN, ENGLAND, AFRICA, AND SOUTH AMERICA. AND EACH STATE, PROVINCE OR REGION WILL HAVE ITS OWN FILTER CENTER WHICH WILL SERVE AS A RECEIVING STATION FOR ALL UFO REPORTS AND OTHER ASSOCIATED DATA, A DISTRIBUTION POINT FOR MATERIAL FROM HEADQUARTERS, AND AS A GENERAL SECRETARY FOR ICARF.

OUR FILTER-OBSERVER NETWORK IS EXPANDING BY LEAPS AND BOUNDS, BUT WE ARE STILL FAR FROM OUR GOAL.

THE AIR FORCE INVESTIGATION HAS EITHER GONE UNDERGROUND, OR ELSE THEY HAVE THE ANSWER AND ARE HIDING THE FACT FROM THE PUBLIC. THE FACT REMAINS THAT MILLIONS OF PEOPLE CONTINUE TO SEE THESE UFOS AND THE PUBLIC STILL DOESN'T KNOW WHAT THEY ARE.

IT IS OUR PURPOSE IN ORGANIZING ICARF TO FIND THE ANSWER TO THE UFO MYSTERY, AND GIVE THE FACTS TO THE PUBLIC. AND IN ORDER TO DO THIS WE WILL NEED THE ASSISTANCE OF EVERY PERSON CONCERNED. PERSONS WITH MOVIE AND STILL CAMERAS ARE PARTICULARLY NEEDED, FOR THE EMPHASIS IS ON PHOTOGRAPHIC DATA, DESPITE THE FACT THAT SIGHTINGS WITH BINOCULARS AND TELESCOPES, SOUND DETECTOR AND HAM RADIO STATIONS ARE ALSO VERY IMPORTANT IN THE PROCESS OF OUR RESEARCH.

ALL PERSONS INTERESTED IN SETTING UP OBSERVER POSTS WHO CAN DEVOTE FROM ONE TO THREE HOURS A DAY ON SKYWATCH, AND WHO CAN BE RELIED UPON TO HAVE THEIR CAMERAS LOADED AND READY TO PHOTOGRAPH THESE OBJECTS SHOULD IMMEDIATELY CONTACT US FOR FULL INFORMATION. ADULT SKYWATCH CLUBS, HIGH SCHOOL GROUPS, SCIENCE CLUBS, INDUSTRIAL GROUPS, AND INDIVIDUALS IN GENERAL ARE ALL PARTICULARLY ASKED TO JOIN US IN OUR SEARCH FOR THE ANSWER TO THE UFOS.

CONTACT US AT THE ADDRESS ABOVE.

INTER CONTINENTAL AERIAL RESEARCH
FOUNDATION

FORM OP-2                                                                    62-83894-289

cover_up_reasoning: The most significant indicator of concealment is the handwritten 'DESTROY' instruction with a large red X on the first page, suggesting an intent to remove the file from circulation. This is partially offset by a 'DO NOT DESTROY' stamp and the eventual FOIPA release. There are also minor redactions of names and locations, but no extensive black-bar redactions or references to classified annexes in the narrative.
summary_zh: 联邦调查局案件档案62-HQ-83894的本节包含1957年末至1958年初的内部备忘录。其中详细记载了古巴境内关于飞行碟的报告，这些飞行碟被描述为无声且呈帽状，并概述了1957年10月至11月期间美国境内的其他目击事件，包括形似月亮并带有蓝色火焰的物体、火球状物体以及导致汽车引擎停转的物体。空军将这些美国境内的目击事件定性为“不真实”。该文件还涵盖了联邦调查局对“洲际航空研究基金会”（ICARF）的审查，这是一个寻求公众协助和不明飞行物照片证据的民间组织，该组织认为空军正在隐瞒信息。联邦调查局决定不再对ICARF进行进一步调查。
reported_object_description_zh: 飞行碟，帽状，大于普通飞机，完全无声，形似月亮，拖曳着蓝色火焰，状如心脏跳动，火球状物体，巨大物体。
reported_behavior_zh: 悬停，以极高速度消失，着陆，飞向太空，划过南部天空，飞越，导致汽车引擎停转。
official_resolution_zh: 空军调查了犹他州弗纳尔的报告，结果为阴性。空军将美国境内的其他严重目击事件定性为“不真实或无目击依据”。联邦调查局未对具体报告或洲际航空研究基金会采取进一步行动。
bust_reasoning_zh: 该文件包含数份耸人听闻的报告，包括无声、高速移动的碟状物和所谓的着陆事件，但明确指出空军将美国境内的此类案件“定性为不真实或无目击依据”。古巴的报告虽然描述了异常物体，但仅为未经调查的报纸剪报。文件中没有多传感器佐证或官方调查来证实异常行为，且空军内部对美国案件的驳斥是导致“破除神话”评分较高的一个重要因素。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 最显著的隐瞒迹象是首页上带有大红色X的“销毁”手写指令，表明意图将该文件从流通中移除。这一点被“请勿销毁”的印章和最终的《信息自由法》发布部分抵消。此外，文件对姓名和地点进行了少量编辑，但叙述中没有大面积的黑条遮盖或提及机密附件。
claims: Two flying discs, described as hat-shaped, silent, and larger than airplanes, were sighted hovering and disappearing at high speed in Pinar del Rio and Matanzas, Cuba, in November 1957.

Reports of an object "like the moon" with a "blue flame" landing in Vernal, Utah, and other US sightings (object landing in Nebraska with six occupants, fiery object across southern skies, huge object over Gulf of Mexico, UFOs stopping car engines) were deemed non-authentic by the Air Force.
The Inter Continental Aerial Research Foundation (ICARF) is establishing a global network of UFO observer posts to collect photographic and other data.
ICARF claims the Air Force is either investigating UFOs secretly or hiding the answer from the public.
claims_zh: 1957年11月，在古巴比那尔德里奥和马坦萨斯，目击到两个飞行碟，被描述为帽状、无声且大于飞机，它们曾悬停并以高速消失。
关于犹他州弗纳尔有“形似月亮”并带有“蓝色火焰”的物体着陆的报告，以及美国境内的其他目击事件（内布拉斯加州有六名乘员的物体着陆，火球状物体划过南部天空，墨西哥湾上空有巨大物体，不明飞行物导致汽车引擎停转）均被空军认定为不真实。
洲际航空研究基金会（ICARF）正在建立一个全球不明飞行物观察站网络，以收集照片及其他数据。
ICARF声称空军要么正在秘密调查不明飞行物，要么正在向公众隐瞒真相。
bust_explanations: Misidentification of conventional aircraft or natural phenomena: The 'flying disc' descriptions could be misinterpretations of distant aircraft, weather phenomena, or even hoaxes reported in newspapers. The 'fiery object' is a classic description of a meteor or re-entering space debris.

Hoax or exaggeration in newspaper reports: The Cuban reports are from newspapers, which can sometimes sensationalize or report unverified claims. The Nebraska landing with 'six people aboard' sounds highly sensational and was deemed 'nonauthentic' by the Air Force.

Psychological phenomena/mass hysteria: The memo on page 6 notes a 'great increase in the number of "flying saucers"' after Sputnik, suggesting a psychological component to some sightings, especially given the lack of physical evidence found.
bust_explanations_zh: 对常规飞行器或自然现象的误认：“飞行碟”的描述可能是对远处飞机、天气现象甚至报纸报道的骗局的误解。“火球状物体”是对流星或再入大气层空间碎片的典型描述。
报纸报道中的骗局或夸大：古巴的报告来源于报纸，报纸有时会进行煽情报道或报告未经证实的主张。内布拉斯加州“载有六人”的着陆事件听起来极具煽动性，并被空军认定为“不真实”。
心理现象/群体性歇斯底里：第6页的备忘录指出，在“伴侣号”卫星发射后，“飞碟”数量“大幅增加”，这表明一些目击事件存在心理因素，特别是考虑到缺乏物理证据。
cover_up_indicators: Handwritten 'DESTROY' instruction with large red X on page 1
Minor redactions of names and locations (e.g., SAC, [illegible]; 'shape of a [illegible]')
cover_up_indicators_zh: 第1页上带有大红色X的“销毁”手写指令
姓名和地点的少量编辑（例如，SAC，[无法辨认]；“一个[无法辨认]的形状”）
